name: Selank
subtitle: Anxiolytic & Nootropic Peptide | Tuftsin Analog

modifications: Synthetic analog of tuftsin with anxiolytic properties

overview: Selank is a synthetic heptapeptide analog of the naturally occurring immune peptide tuftsin, developed by the Russian Academy of Sciences. It provides anxiolytic effects comparable to benzodiazepines but without sedation, amnesia, tolerance, or withdrawal.
mechanism: Works through allosteric modulation of GABA-A receptors, serotonin and dopamine system optimization, enkephalin degradation inhibition, IL-6 and cytokine balance regulation, and BDNF upregulation in the hippocampus.

items.name: Generalized Anxiety Disorder
items.description: Clinical trials show significant reduction in anxiety symptoms comparable to benzodiazepines but without sedation or amnesia.

items.name: Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder
items.description: Research indicates effectiveness in PTSD treatment.

items.name: Social Anxiety
items.description: Reduces anticipatory anxiety and performance stress.

items.name: Memory Enhancement
items.description: Improves memory consolidation and learning capacity.

items.name: Attention and Focus
items.description: Enhanced sustained attention during demanding cognitive tasks.

items.name: Neuroprotection
items.description: BDNF upregulation supports long-term brain health.

items.name: Immune Modulation
items.description: Balances immune system function through cytokine regulation.

items.name: Antiviral Activity
items.description: Shows activity against influenza, HSV, and cytomegalovirus.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection is the primary method with well-documented protocols. Provides reliable absorption and dosing.

steps: Remove vial from refrigeration; allow 10-15 minutes to reach room temperature
Clean vial tops with alcohol wipes using circular motion; air dry
Calculate reconstitution volume for desired concentration
Draw bacteriostatic water into insulin syringe; remove air bubbles
Insert needle at 45-degree angle against glass wall
Slowly inject BAC water down vial side to minimize foaming
Remove needle and gently swirl vial (never shake vigorously)
Allow 1-2 minutes if cloudiness remains; swirl until clear
Verify final solution is clear and colorless
Label with reconstitution date and concentration

deliveryMethods.overview: Intranasal spray provides fastest onset (15-30 minutes) and is non-invasive. Popular for as-needed anxiety relief.

interactions.notes: Enhanced cognitive and anxiolytic effects when combined.
Both enhance BDNF via different pathways.
Balanced anxiety relief and cognition enhancement.
Similar GABAergic effects; medical supervision advised.
Can combine for enhanced mood regulation; monitor closely.
Both affect GABA system; risk of excessive sedation.

timeline.effects: Enhanced stress resilience and emotional stability

common: Minimal side effects - excellent safety profile
stopSigns: Severe allergic reactions (rash, breathing difficulty, swelling)
Persistent injection site reactions or infection signs
Unusual mood changes or increased anxiety
contraindications: Consult healthcare provider with multiple psychiatric medications

good: White to off-white lyophilized powder without clumping
Clear batch information, purity testing, expiration dates
warning: Unusual smell or burning sensation may indicate bacterial contamination
Room temperature shipping of nasal spray

references.title: GABAergic Gene Expression Study
references.keyFindings: Rat frontal cortex study at 300mcg/kg showed modulation of 45 genes involved in neurotransmission; GABAergic mechanism confirmed.

references.title: GAD Clinical Trial
references.keyFindings: Human subjects showed significant anxiolytic and mild nootropic effects lasting up to one week post-administration.

references.title: BDNF Enhancement Study
references.keyFindings: Rat hippocampus study at 250-500mcg/kg showed 30% increase in hippocampal BDNF levels.

references.title: Memory Consolidation Study
references.keyFindings: Significant activation of learning processes in subjects with poor initial learning.

latestResearch.title: Inflammation-Related Gene Expression Modulation
latestResearch.summary: New findings on Selank's effects on inflammatory gene expression.

latestResearch.title: Comparison with Classical Benzodiazepines
latestResearch.summary: Comparative study showing similar efficacy without benzodiazepine side effects.

latestResearch.title: Antiviral Activity Study
latestResearch.summary: Demonstrated activity against influenza, HSV, and cytomegalovirus.
